steps.title: Find the main verb
steps.content: The sentence already has a main verb: consist. The blank appears between the subject, Trisyllabic words, and the rest of the sentence. It should modify the subject, not create another finite verb phrase that competes with the main verb.

steps.title: Use a participle modifier
steps.content: The participle classified works as a modifier in the phrase words classified as dactyls. B, C, and D each add a finite verb phrase before consist, leaving the sentence with two competing main verbs. Only the modifier form keeps the sentence grammatical. The answer is A.
